pundit.name: Clint Dempsey
pundit.take: "**Mustard Boy Zach's on a hot streak, and Ghost Pepper Devin's feelin' the heat before USA faces Turkey.** Now, Zach right now is on fire like a Texan summer, ridin' high after Brazil cooked up a 3-0 win over Scotland. Mustard Boy's Saudi Arabia pick still cracks me up though — thinkin' oil money was gonna get him goals? Wishful thinkin', partner. On the flip side, Ghost Pepper Devin's got USA goin' up against The Backpass Assassin Gayden's Turkey. With Turkey still tryin' to find their groove, Devin's backing USA to handle biz in Cali, but Gayden still got England, and ya never know when he'll backpass his way into an upset. But the real kicker? Gayden out here draftin' Jordan like they could be world beaters. Let's keep it real: I'm backin' USA to serve up a dish of humble pie to Turkey faster than a Texas BBQ cook-off."

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Teams Set To Face Challenges In Crucial Matches Today
pundit_takes.subtitle: "The Backpass Assassin" drafts English-speaking nations; fluency not translating to wins.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin's USA Prepared To Handle Turkey, But By How Much?
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper's spicy lineup ready; just don't expect a gourmet performance.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Rides Father's Coattails, Climbs Standings Without Qualified Teams
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker making more noise off the pitch than on it.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Brazil Dominance Confirmed With Clinical Win Over Scotland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy's strategic ketchups proving effective against bland opponents.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach's in first, but that Czechia experiment makes me question his decision-making skills.
Rafe's Tier-1 dependency is higher than his math grades, and that's saying something.
Rafe and Gayden are tied. Happy Father's Day, Backpass Assassin!
Gunner's favorite odds dropped faster than his ability to name his Senegal players.
